channel.title: Energy & Environment
channel.description: News, Analysis and Opinion from POLITICO

item.title: Ukrainian energy official: Lifting Russia oil sanctions is ‘wrong approach’
item.description: Oleksii Riabchyn, chief international officer at Naftogaz, also described Ukraine's efforts to position itself as a major natural gas hub for Europe.

media:content.media:description: Naftogaz executive talks Ukranian energy potential and impact of Russian attacks
media:content.media:title: Naftogaz executive talks Ukranian energy potential and impact of Russian attacks

item.dc:creator: By James Bikales

item.title: Governors forgo past response to high gasoline prices
item.description: State leaders in both parties say gas tax holidays did little to help consumers the last time a war shook the oil market. They see even less reason to try them now.

media:content.media:title: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis speaks last year during a news conference in Miramar, Florida.

item.dc:creator: By Adam Aton

item.title: US must reopen Strait of Hormuz to cut spiking energy prices, oil chief says
item.description: American Petroleum Institute CEO Mike Sommers says industry leaders want to "make sure that we're finishing the job in Iran."

media:content.media:description: API CEO Mike Sommers talks Iran, state of the U.S. oil industry and more
media:content.media:title: API CEO Mike Sommers talks Iran, state of the U.S. oil industry and more

item.dc:creator: By Mike Lee

item.title: Machado: Venezuela could quintuple oil production under a democratic government
item.description: The Venezuelan opposition leader laid out a detailed plan to oil executives gathered in Houston that would transform Venezuela's oil sector from a state-run enterprise to a 'fully private' industry.

media:content.media:title: Maria Corina Machado said her plan could raise the country’s oil production from the current roughly 1 million barrels a day to as much as 5 million barrels a day, higher than the country produced at its peak.

item.dc:creator: By James Bikales

item.title: Burgum: Iran energy price shock a matter of weeks, not months
item.description: Interior Secretary Doug Burgum stressed President Donald Trump is 'empathetic' about rising prices.

media:content.media:description: Interior Sec. Burgum: Trump 'empathetic' to rising energy prices
media:content.media:title: Interior Sec. Burgum: Trump 'empathetic' to rising energy prices

item.dc:creator: By Ben Lefebvre

item.title: ‘The worst I’ve seen’: Oil industry grapples with the fallout from US-Israel war with Iran
item.description: The Middle East war is scrambling markets and threatening to remake the energy industry.

media:content.media:title: A speedboat operated by members of Iran's Revolutionary Guard approaches a British-flagged oil tanker in the Strait of Hormuz in 2019.

item.dc:creator: By James Bikales and Ben Lefebvre
item.dc:contributor: By James Bikales and Ben Lefebvre

item.title: Ending Iran war now would cede Hormuz to the enemy, Trump’s former Defense secretary says
item.description: "You’d see a tax for every ship that goes through" Hormuz if Trump quits now, Gen. Jim Mattis said.

media:content.media:title: The assessment from former Gen. Jim Mattis comes as President Donald Trump declared a five-day pause on military strikes against Iranian energy infrastructure after the U.S. and Israeli attack against Iran enters its fourth week.

item.dc:creator: By Ben Lefebvre

item.title: ‘The opportunity is here’: TerraPower CEO credits NRC for moving quickly on projects
item.description: TerraPower recently became the first company to secure a permit from the NRC to construct a full, commercial advanced nuclear reactor.

media:content.media:description: TerraPower CEO says it's 'unfortunate' that nuclear isn't ready to meet AI demand
media:content.media:title: TerraPower CEO says it's 'unfortunate' that nuclear isn't ready to meet AI demand

item.dc:creator: By Kelsey Tamborrino and Francisco "A.J." Camacho
item.dc:contributor: By Kelsey Tamborrino and Francisco "A.J." Camacho

item.title: White House energy adviser says oil companies ‘all on board’ with upping production
item.description: Jarrod Agen, the executive director of the White House National Energy Dominance Council, said he has not received pushback to the administration's calls to drill more amid the war in Iran.

media:content.media:description: Jarrod Agen on Iran strikes: 'President Trump knows exactly what he's doing'
media:content.media:title: Jarrod Agen on Iran strikes: 'President Trump knows exactly what he's doing'

item.dc:creator: By James Bikales

item.title: Trump administration, energy developer announce end of U.S. offshore wind projects
item.description: The announcement marks the latest blow by the administration against the U.S. offshore wind industry.

media:content.media:title: Interior Secretary Doug Burgum smiles during a meeting with Venezuela's acting President Delcy Rodríguez on Wednesday at Miraflores presidential palace in Caracas.

item.dc:creator: By Kelsey Tamborrino

item.title: US-Iran war damaged global oil markets more than Russia-Ukraine war, Chevron CEO says
item.description: Mike Wirth said it will take some time to rebuild inventories and supply chains after the Strait of Hormuz fully opens.

media:content.media:title: Chevron CEO Mike Wirth in 2021.

item.dc:creator: By Mike Lee

item.title: Wright urges oil and gas industry to up production amid Iran war
item.description: “Markets do what markets do,” he said at the CERAWeek by S&P Global conference in Houston.

media:content.media:title: Energy Secretary Chris Wright listens in the Cabinet Room of the White House on Oct. 14, 2025.

item.dc:creator: By James Bikales and Kelsey Tamborrino
item.dc:contributor: By James Bikales and Kelsey Tamborrino

item.title: Why Texas isn’t cheering oil price spike
item.description: Industry executives are gathering in the U.S. energy capital this week as uncertainty mounts and crude costs soar.

media:content.media:title: Oil storage containers in Houston, which is hosting the CERAWeek by S&P Global conference this week amid high oil prices.

item.dc:creator: By Mike Lee

item.title: 'When will it end?’ The ‘elevator pitch’ oil executives and diplomats are making to the White House in Houston
item.description: At the world’s largest energy industry gathering, there’s one question on top of everyone’s minds.

media:content.media:title: Fire and plumes of smoke rise after a drone struck a fuel tank near Dubai International Airport in the United Arab Emirates on March 16.

item.dc:creator: By Ben Lefebvre, Phelim Kine and Mike Soraghan
item.dc:contributor: By Ben Lefebvre, Phelim Kine and Mike Soraghan

item.title: Cuba’s power grid collapses leaving it without electricity for the 3rd time this month
item.description: The island nation's infrastructure is in decay.

media:content.media:title: People spend the night on the Malecon during a blackout in Havana, Cuba, on March 21, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Associated Press

item.title: You can bet on climate disasters. Business is booming.
item.description: Traders are placing wagers on the likelihood of disasters, activists being jailed and oil depots exploding.

media:content.media:title: Hurricane Michael was the last Category 5 storm to hit the U.S. in 2018. Bettors are increasingly wagering money on climate-related events.

item.dc:creator: By Corbin Hiar

item.title: US pauses sanctions on some of Iran’s oil as gas prices surge
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the U.S. will allow the sale of shipments already in transit.

media:content.media:title: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent addresses journalists during a news conference at the OECD Headquarters on March 16 in Paris, France.

item.dc:creator: By Aaron Pellish

item.title: The states where higher gas prices could shape the midterms
item.description: The map of price hikes shows highest increases are mostly in states that lean Republican.

media:content.media:title: Gasoline prices in Texas are up $1 per gallon since the start of the U.S. war with Iran.

item.dc:creator: By Catherine Allen

item.title: ‘Hormuz has to reopen’: Why the oil industry can’t help Trump tame rising gasoline prices
item.description: Oil industry executives say much of the administration’s messaging on supply disruptions from the Iran war has discouraged drilling.

media:content.media:title: Oil pump jacks are seen in a field in Stanton, Texas.

item.dc:creator: By Zack Colman, James Bikales and Mike Soraghan
item.dc:contributor: By Zack Colman, James Bikales and Mike Soraghan

item.title: Trump officials rule out oil export ban in meeting with industry execs
item.description: U.S. benchmark West Texas Intermediate crude climbed during the session, crossing $101 per barrel from $67 per barrel before the war.

media:content.media:title: Interior Secretary Doug Burgum (left) and Energy Secretary Chris Wright deliver remarks outside the White House on March 19, 2025, in Washington.

item.dc:creator: By Sophia Cai and Ben Lefebvre
item.dc:contributor: By Sophia Cai and Ben Lefebvre

item.title: Trump administration may unsanction some Iranian oil as energy prices spike, Bessent says
item.description: The Treasury secretary said the Trump administration has several “levers” to pull as war in the Middle East roils oil markets.

media:content.media:title: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent arrives to attend an event for Inter Miami CF, winners of the 2025 Major League Soccer Cup, in the East Room of the White House in Washington on March 5.

item.dc:creator: By Gregory Svirnovskiy and Ben Lefebvre
item.dc:contributor: By Gregory Svirnovskiy and Ben Lefebvre

item.title: Trump is poised to take Iran’s Kharg Island. Here’s what could unfold next.
item.description: The president’s attacks on Iran’s oil infrastructure could determine the course of the war — and its domestic political fallout.

media:content.media:title: President Donald Trump arrives Wednesday at Joint Base Andrews after attending a ceremony for six Air Force crew members who died when their refueling plane crashed in western Iraq last week.

item.dc:creator: By Scott Waldman

item.title: Vance, Wright to address oil execs amid Iran tensions
item.description: The meeting comes as oil and gasoline prices have spiked after Iran closed a key waterway for global oil shipments.

media:content.media:title: Vice President JD Vance will meet with oil executives this week at the American Petroleum Institute board meeting.

item.dc:creator: By Zack Colman and James Bikales
item.dc:contributor: By Zack Colman and James Bikales

item.title: How Canada's embrace of Chinese EVs could scramble the American market
item.description: Canada's import plans reverse years of North American policy that treated the cheap, high-tech Chinese cars as an economic threat.

media:content.media:title: Xiaomi CEO Lei Jun introduces the YU7 car in Beijing last year. Canada will soon start importing Chinese electric vehicles.

item.dc:creator: By David Ferris

item.title: The National Park Service race to rewrite history becomes a slog
item.description: The agency is considering hundreds of changes to exhibits at parks. It’s been an overwhelming task.

media:content.media:title: Exhibits discussing slavery and the Founding Fathers' ownership of enslaved people are seen at the President's House at Independence National Historic Park on Aug. 9, 2025, in Philadelphia. The National Park Service in January took down the exhibit to comply with an order by President Donald Trump, although it was later partially reinstalled.

item.dc:creator: By Heather Richards

item.title: Energy Secretary Chris Wright says Iran war will likely end in weeks
item.description: He has repeatedly dismissed rising oil prices as “a short-term disruption.”

media:content.media:title: A man stands atop rubble as smoke rises from a building destroyed in an Israeli airstrike in Dahiyeh, Lebanon, on March 14 as part of the U.S. and Israel's ongoing war with Iran.

item.dc:creator: By Jacob Wendler

item.title: Trump administration invokes emergency powers to restart oil operations off California coast
item.description: The Energy Department cited U.S. military dependence on foreign oil.

media:content.media:title: Energy Secretary Chris Wright on Friday issued an order that will have a significant impact on California's oil industry.

item.dc:creator: By Noah Baustin

item.title: ‘Oh, boy’: Oil industry frets over Trump's profit-minded Iran post
item.description: Trump’s social-media post is a potential public relations hurdle for an industry that has a complicated relationship with the president.

media:content.media:credit: Royal Thai Navy/AFP via Getty Images
media:content.media:title: Attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz are snarling oil-tanker traffic — and sending prices soaring.

item.dc:creator: By Ben Lefebvre, James Bikales and Carlos Anchondo
item.dc:contributor: By Ben Lefebvre, James Bikales and Carlos Anchondo

item.title: Five reasons oil prices won't snap back from Iran war
item.description: Tanker backlogs, damaged energy infrastructure and threats in the Strait of Hormuz could keep gasoline prices elevated.

media:content.media:title: A man walks along the shore in the United Arab Emirates as oil tankers and cargo ships line up in the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday.

item.dc:creator: By Scott Waldman

item.title: White House considers waiving maritime commerce restrictions as fuel prices skyrocket
item.description: The move comes as the U.S. and Israel’s war in Iran has sent oil prices soaring, with a key choke point effectively blocked.

media:content.media:credit: Frederic J. Brown/AFP via Getty Images
media:content.media:title: High gas prices are listed at Chevron gas station in Los Angeles on Monday as gasoline prices surge amid the ongoing war with Iran.

item.dc:creator: By Jacob Wendler
